---
title: "Data Format"
---

ferx-core reads data in NONMEM-compatible CSV format. This is the standard
format shared by NONMEM, Monolix, and most population PK tools.

## Required columns

| Column | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `ID` | string/numeric | Subject identifier |
| `TIME` | numeric | Time relative to first dose |
| `DV` | numeric | Dependent variable (observed concentration) |

## Optional standard columns

| Column | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `EVID` | integer | 0 | Event ID — see table below |
| `AMT` | numeric | 0 | Dose amount (dose rows only) |
| `CMT` | integer | 1 | Compartment number (1-indexed) |
| `RATE` | numeric | 0 | Infusion rate; 0 = bolus |
| `MDV` | integer | 0 | Missing DV flag; 1 = skip this observation |
| `II` | numeric | 0 | Interdose interval for steady-state records |
| `SS` | integer | 0 | Steady-state flag; 1 = assume steady state before TIME=0 |
| `CENS` | integer | 0 | BLOQ flag; 1 = DV is below LLOQ (DV carries the LLOQ value) |

Missing values may be represented as `.` or as an empty cell.

Column names are case-insensitive: `ID`, `Id`, and `id` are all recognised.

## Event types (EVID)

| EVID | Meaning |
|---|---|
| 0 | Observation record. `DV` is used for estimation. |
| 1 | Dose record. `AMT` is administered to compartment `CMT`. |
| 2 | Covariate-change marker. Compartment amounts are unchanged but the PK parameter expressions are re-evaluated using this row's covariate values. No-op when all covariates are time-constant. |
| 4 | Reset + dose. All compartment amounts are zeroed before dosing. |

## Covariate columns

Any column not in the standard set above is treated as a covariate.

- **Time-constant covariates**: the first non-missing value per subject is used.
- **Time-varying covariates**: Last Observation Carried Forward (LOCF) per row —
  individual-parameter expressions are re-evaluated at each dose and observation
  row using that row's covariate snapshot.

Covariate names are matched case-insensitively against names in
`[individual_parameters]` expressions. Numeric covariate columns that are not
the IOV occasion column are candidates for `ferx_eta_cov()` in the R layer.

### Time-varying covariate support

Time-varying covariates are supported for all analytical models (1/2/3-cpt) and
all ODE-defined models. The Enzyme gradient fast path is also event-driven for
TV-covariate subjects on analytical models — the AD path does not fall back to
finite differences when covariates vary.

## Occasion column (IOV)

When using Inter-Occasion Variability, add an integer occasion-index column and
name it with `iov_column` in `[fit_options]`:

```
[fit_options]
  iov_column = OCC
```

The `OCC` column:

- Contains **positive integer occasion indices** (e.g. 1, 2, 3…) — one per row
- Applies to both dose and observation rows
- Is excluded from covariate auto-detection
- Does not need to start at 1 or be consecutive; different numbers mean
  different occasions with independent kappa EBEs

## BLOQ observations

Set `CENS = 1` on any row where the measured concentration is below the lower
limit of quantification. The `DV` column must contain the **LLOQ value** (not a
zero or missing). Pair with `bloq_method = m3` in `[fit_options]` to enable
Beal M3 likelihood-based BLOQ handling; the default `drop` simply discards these
rows.

## Infusion doses

Set `RATE > 0` on a dose row to specify an infusion. Duration is derived as
`AMT / RATE`:

```csv
ID,TIME,DV,EVID,AMT,CMT,RATE,MDV
1,0,.,1,500,1,50,1
```

This administers 500 units over 10 hours at a rate of 50 units/hour.

**Dose and infusion routing** on analytical models:

| CMT | IV models | Oral models |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Central compartment | Depot — bolus is absorbed via first-order KA |
| 2 | Peripheral 1 infusion (2-cpt and 3-cpt) | Central compartment infusion |
| 3 | Peripheral 2 infusion (3-cpt only) | — (panics with a clear message) |

## Steady-state dosing

Set `SS = 1` and `II` to the dosing interval on a dose row to instruct
ferx-core to compute the compartment amounts that would result from an infinite
history of doses spaced `II` apart before `TIME = 0`:

```csv
ID,TIME,DV,EVID,AMT,CMT,SS,II,MDV
1,0,.,1,100,1,1,12,1
1,0.5,25.3,0,.,.,.,.,0
```

## Multiple doses

Each dose is a separate row with `EVID = 1`. Analytical models sum contributions
via multi-dose superposition:

```csv
ID,TIME,DV,EVID,AMT,CMT,MDV
1,0,.,1,100,1,1
1,0.5,9.49,0,.,.,0
1,12,.,1,100,1,1
1,12.5,15.2,0,.,.,0
1,24,.,1,100,1,1
1,24.5,18.1,0,.,.,0
```

## Full example with covariates

```csv
ID,TIME,DV,EVID,AMT,CMT,RATE,MDV,WT,CRCL
1,0,.,1,100,1,0,1,70,95
1,0.5,9.49,0,.,.,.,0,70,95
1,1,14.42,0,.,.,.,0,70,95
1,2,17.56,0,.,.,.,0,70,95
1,4,15.23,0,.,.,.,0,70,95
1,8,10.15,0,.,.,.,0,70,95
2,0,.,1,150,1,0,1,85,110
2,0.5,14.2,0,.,.,.,0,85,110
2,1,21.3,0,.,.,.,0,85,110
```

- Dose records (`EVID=1`): `MDV=1`, `DV=.`
- Observation records (`EVID=0`): `MDV=0`, valid `DV`
- Covariates (`WT`, `CRCL`) are extra columns; matched to `[individual_parameters]` by name

## Relevant source files

| File | Role |
|---|---|
| `io/datareader.rs` | `read_nonmem_csv()` — full CSV parser, LOCF, covariate detection |
| `types.rs` | `Subject`, `DoseEvent`, `Population` — parsed representation |
